entities.description: case b refers to a scenario involving an existing entity where an alias is being evaluated for relevance
the reranker is a system or component described as having inherent discriminative limitations
existing entity refers to an entity that is already present in the system being discussed
alias is a term used to describe an alternative name for an existing entity
scores are the numerical values indicating the relevance of an alias to a true match
true match refers to the correct entity that an alias is being compared against
equivalent aliases refers to a small set of aliases that are considered the same as the true match
gradient refers to the sharp decline in relevance scores mentioned in the text
irrelevant entities are the entities that follow the sharp decline in relevance scores

relations.description: is the specific alias being discussed within the scenario defined as case b
the scores of     are influenced by the inherent discriminative limitations of the reranker
case b is defined by the scenario involving an existing entity
case b specifically addresses the situation where an alias is being evaluated
the alias is evaluated for its relevance to the true match
scores indicate the relevance of the alias to the true match
scores show high relevance to the true match or a set of equivalent aliases
the reranker s limitations affect the initial set of high relevance scores
the scores exhibit a sharp decline  gradient  after the initial high relevance set
the gradient precedes the transition to irrelevant entities
